title: lavatera thuringiaca var. cachemiriana
intro: Lavatera thuringiaca var. cachemiriana, commonly known as the Indian rose mallow, is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the hibiscus family. It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is said to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and aiding in wound healing. The leaves and flowers of the plant can be used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and topical creams, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
